title: Stellar Remnants Encountered
query: SELECT COUNT(*) AS count FROM Scan WHERE StarType IN ('N', 'H', 'D', 'DA', 'DAB', 'DAO', 'DAZ', 'DAV', 'DB', 'DBZ', 'DBV', 'DO', 'DOV', 'DQ', 'DC', 'DCV', 'DX') AND event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate
detailQuery: SELECT CASE WHEN StarType = 'N' THEN 'Neutron Star' WHEN StarType = 'H' THEN 'Black Hole' ELSE 'White Dwarf' END AS label, COUNT(*) AS value FROM Scan WHERE StarType IN ('N', 'H', 'D', 'DA', 'DAB', 'DAO', 'DAZ', 'DAV', 'DB', 'DBZ', 'DBV', 'DO', 'DOV', 'DQ', 'DC', 'DCV', 'DX') AND event_timestamp >= :startDate AND event_timestamp < :endDate GROUP BY label ORDER BY value DESC

details.text: {count} stellar remnant(s) scanned during the report period.
details.help: StarType codes: N=Neutron Star, H=Black Hole, D/DA/DB/DC etc.=White Dwarf variants. summaryOnly: bar chart reduces to a single entry per system.

tagLines: Exotic stellar phenomena survey data archived. The astrophysics division acknowledges your contribution.
Neutron stars, black holes, white dwarfs — the universe's most dramatic last acts.
These are the embers of dead suns. Each one a graveyard that still has its own gravity.
